reasoning: The market probability was around 90% in early April. On April 5, 2025, a 10% "universal tariff" went into effect on all imports, according to numerous news reports (CNN, Guardian, CBS). While Trump subsequently paused his higher "reciprocal tariffs" for most nations on April 9, the 10% baseline universal tariff remained in place. In addition, the US imposed a 125% tariff on imports from China. Because the 10% universal tariff applies to all (or nearly all) goods entering the United States, the weighted average tariff (customs duties divided by import volume) will heavily reflect this baseline. Given that the 10% tariff began just a few days into Q2 2025, it applies to the vast majority of the quarter's imports. Mathematically, it is exceedingly probable that the weighted average tariff easily surpasses the 6% threshold required for this market to resolve YES, either in Q2 or later in 2025. I assign a 94% probability, leaving only a small margin for potential swift legal injunctions, policy reversals, or measurement quirks in the FRED data.
